if you're using, looking for something, there's, like, an actual widget here that works, right?
Or the work that we're doing for Lovable on the template side, there's the app, and, you know, And so if you...
Use this template.
It's going to remix and pull the history and we use their API.
So that's what we're doing with exploring that with Webflow.
We're talking to Zapier as well.

And all of those include like integrations into some kind of API, some kind of data set or something that that gives them an information gain and experience gain and connects into the product experience as well.
Right.

Are you saying with those elements, it won't be seen by the algorithm as programmatic content as much and avoiding maybe- thing is, with those elements, you're optimizing for a great experience for the users, no matter what.
And if you do that, Google and everybody else, that's what they're optimizing for is great experiences, right?
So if someone goes to a page and it's shallow, they don't spend a lot of time and work out.
But I don't know, like it's a black box, a lot of these things, right?
And so it's not a clear cut, like do this for sure, because there's exceptions to everything.
Ayo, you never have.

With that 100% certainty, and I hear you on programmatic in and of itself not being something that's penalized, but what I'm trying to understand is, if you could summarize how your clients fared in December, would you say it was neutral, positive, negative?
What's kind of the TLDR there?
There's just no way to kind of summarize everything because we have not seen anything crazy out of the ordinary, but it's really from client to client, right?

Like, so there's, like, clients that are in the corporate card and expense management that have experienced sometimes, like, their ranking stays consistent, but then traffic completely drops on categories that we're driving and I've seen a lot of, like, business value to them, right?
And so they've had to essentially make that up in the long tail with a lot of different things.
They're lower intent.
They're not giving them the same thing.
And then over time,
It kind of comes back up to, like, where they were before from a business selling conversion perspective, right?
And so the way we approach it is, like, truly try to understand the product.
So take AugmentCode, their coding agent, right?
And we're talking to Cognition right now as well.
Like, we didn't just do a library of NCP servers.
We actually did a library that eventually was a click away to install, right?
And we made this, like, all, like, programmatic.
But it added value to the fundamental thing that they were trying to use the product for.
And so even if traffic didn't go crazy up to the right, it was the right thing to do in really good value.
Same thing with templates, right?
And so, like, again, like, it's all about, like, I think there's a direct proportion.
Like, you see a brand like Lovable where they have an obscene amount of backlinks and brand love.
And it's kind of like they have so much karma, I think about, right, that, like, they can move really fast and get really aggressive.

And then there's other brands, I think Zapier might fall into that, where they really pushed the envelope for a very, very, very, very, very, very, very long time on things that can be questionable, right?
And even though the content is not complete garbage, I've never read anything on Zapier's blog that truly changed the way I thought about something, right?
Oh, I think their content is excellent.
I thought we were talking more about like the No, no, I'm talking about the listicle, like the tens, the like thousands and thousands of listicles that they have, right?
Like the best of, the best this, the best that, like those listicles are like, let me rephrase that because that came off a little rough, like because I love Zapier.
And it's like those listicles are not necessarily the best resource if you're trying to buy in a category.
They might give you a list and it's a good starting place, but, you know, when you go too heavy, I think in some of these, it can be kind of like, like for a very long time.
It's hard to be.

You get so much exposure to, and, you know, some of these algo changes can impact them, but I don't know, like, I don't, quite honestly, I don't think I've spent enough time really thinking about algo changes.
What I always spend time thinking about the audiences we're trying to serve, and how do I just become the best resource for them all the time?
And I just find that, like, that adds up eventually, you know?
And it's a better place of focus than, like, what an engineer at Google decided to do, which they're not even going to tell you anyways, you know?
Totally fair.
Yeah.
But I feel like I've been talking for a long time, so apologies if it's, like, I'm throwing too much at you, but hopefully that helps you kind of see the big picture.
The biggest challenges we had back in the day before I started GrowthX when I was using Aerofs was that, like, in any local tool for that matter.
is, like, it's not based in code, right?
And so when you're building AI features and pipelines that are not based in code, models are changing so fast.
And it's really hard.
Like, I'll give you a very specific example.
I started running into things where factually incorrect information, right?
So then I had to go myself, because I built those pipelines dragging and dropping, I had to figure out exactly where to go in this cobble web to go fix that, right?
But then I realized it's not fixable.

So then instead of what we need to do is, like, chunk the content into blocks and then go run a deep research for each block to go figure out if that, like, was accurate or not and use a bunch of logic in that, right?
That alone is, like, an insane workflow that literally broke Aerox at the time because one of those would clog up the system for a whole week to run it on one pipeline for one client, right?
Like, so sometimes we...
That's why we quickly pivoted away from that.

And the reason I'm not to talk about Arup, so I'm going to say it in generic terms, the challenge with any company that was not started from a place of like, I've been doing this for 10 plus years, and that pivoted five, six times to try to capture a market opportunity, is that they're always going to be trying to do just enough to just get the market to buy in a little bit.
But it's not coming from like the conviction of like, what does it take to do this successfully for so many different brands over like 10 plus years, right?

And like for us, like, and as an XCMO, it's like, what we found is like, there's so many instances where the AI workflows and the pipelines are important, but if your context is bad, it's going to be bad, right?
Or if your strategy is bad, you're wasting time, or there are blockers within your website that is just like, man, might...

Team can't prioritize then it's going to take months and months for us to get this design and page ready or hey this is not integrated we need someone to create a database and do this and this needs to pull from that and it needs to be integrated into our product like all those little things ends up being a okay here I go let me go hire yet another agency another vendor another contractor another consultant by another tool and then we you and I and others right end up being just like this orchestrator of a mass of different stakeholders.

And so with growthx we were just trying to like we're not perfect but we're just trying to simplify that so it's like one partner to hold accountable versus like stitching together a bunch of things you know.
Yeah very uh very real pain point well really appreciate you walking me through kind of the the nuts and bolts of the platform um let me sync with Adam on next steps and then we can get back to you.
Yeah yeah yeah that sounds that sounds awesome I mean um you know like one thing I'll say that that it's not like for.
So, like, it's specific to HubSpot is that, like, I would love to, if there's any opportunity, like, for us, this is less about, like, revenue growth, because we're doing fine.
It's really about, like, a company that now has bet on us as well, which, you know, I've looked up to for so many years.

And so, any opportunity to, like, prove ourselves, you know, even as small as it might be, put us to the test, essentially, you know, it would be awesome, because, you know, a ton of respect for you, but also, like, the whole company.
so, that would be pretty awesome to be able to knock it out of the park, even in a small sandbox, you know.
Yeah, totally, totally.
Good to know.
Appreciate that.
And, yeah, it seems like y'all built something pretty impressive, too.
So, the respect is mutual.
I appreciate it.
Well, let me know if anything else comes up, but we're an open book, right?
Like, so, so, anything we can do to be helpful in any other way as well.
We're here for that.
Thanks, Marcel.
Thanks a lot.
Appreciate it, Aja.
Thanks so much.
Bye.
too.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[GrowthX Labs demo for HubSpot's Aja Frost to explore a potential partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**HubSpot's AEO Challenge:** HubSpot's AEO (AI Engine Optimization) is at a "B" maturity level. Key pain points are tracking visibility changes for high-value queries, connecting visibility to actions, and balancing content volume with quality.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=261.0)
  - [**GrowthX's "Context Engineering" Approach:** GrowthX builds content engines by deeply understanding a client's context (product, audience, strategy) and using it to engineer high-quality content at scale, often integrating with product APIs for superior user value.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=488.0)
  - [**The Code-First Workflow Engine:** GrowthX's core differentiator is a code-based workflow engine (open-sourcing next month). This enables complex, multi-agent pipelines (e.g., a 77-minute research agent) that low-code tools like AirOps cannot handle, ensuring superior quality and fact-checking.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1549.0)
  - [**Programmatic Content Strategy:** GrowthX avoids "shallow" programmatic content, which was penalized in the December Google update. Their strategy is to "productize" content by integrating it with client APIs and proprietary data, creating a superior user experience that algorithms reward.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1948.0)

## Topics

### HubSpot's AEO Challenges

  - [**Maturity:** "B" grade, but "A" when graded on a curve.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=275.0)
  - [**Current Tooling:**](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=295.0)
      - [**Technical:** XFunnel (source of truth), Botify, custom bot-specific infrastructure.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=295.0)
      - [**Content:** In-house tooling for mass page creation, AirOps for blog content.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=321.0)
  - [**Primary Pain Points:**](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [**Tracking:** Difficult to isolate visibility changes for high-value queries.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [**Attribution:** Connecting visibility changes to specific actions.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [**Quality vs. Volume:** Balancing the need for high-quality, unique content with the demand for scale.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)

### GrowthX's "Context Engineering" Approach

  - [**Core Philosophy:** Become the "best resource" for an audience by deeply understanding their pain points and use cases, then building an engine to deliver the highest-quality content.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=488.0)
  - [**Methodology:**](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1064.0)
    1.  [**Context:** Deeply understand the client's company, product, market, and audience.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1064.0)
    2.  [**Strategy:** Use context to prioritize opportunities (topics, keywords) based on competitive gaps, brand mentions, and persona needs.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
    3.  [**Execution:** Deploy custom, code-based workflows with human oversight to deliver content.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1221.0)
    4.  [**Optimization:** Ingest signals to prioritize expanding surface area vs. improving existing content.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
  - [**Key Principle:** "Marketing as context engineering." High-quality context is the foundation for high-quality output.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=613.0)

### The Code-First Workflow Engine

  - [**Problem with Low-Code Tools (e.g., AirOps):** Low-code tools cannot handle the complexity required for deep research and fact-checking. A single complex workflow can break the system or run for days.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=2390.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Solution:** A code-based workflow engine built on a file system and coding agents.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1549.0)
      - [**Research Supervisor Agent:** A multi-agent system that plans research, uses deep research APIs, and has an LLM "judge" to evaluate quality against client context.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1563.0)
      - [**Knowledge Base Integration:** Ingests client data (e.g., Webflow's docs) into a graph RAG (Neo4j) for superior semantic retrieval over standard vector databases.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1671.0)
      - [**Human-in-the-Loop:** Strategists and editors provide calibration and feedback to address failure modes, ensuring quality.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1781.0)
  - **Example (Biologica):** For a healthcare client, the engine uses proprietary medical databases and annotates every medical claim for doctor review, reducing review time to \<15 minutes per article.

### Programmatic Content Strategy & Algo Updates

  - [**December Google Update:** The update penalized "shallow" programmatic content (e.g., low-value listicles), not programmatic content itself.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1948.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Strategy:** "Productize" content by integrating it with client APIs or proprietary data to create a superior user experience.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=1971.0)
      - [**Example (Lovable):** A templates library built with their API allows users to "create your own" from a template, accelerating time to value.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=667.0)
      - [**Example (Ramp):** A per diem calculator page provides a functional tool, not just static text.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=876.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Focusing on user value aligns with Google's long-term goals. While traffic may fluctuate, the strategy builds a resilient, high-quality asset.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=2121.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Aja Frost:** Sync with Adam Coccari to define next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=2560.0)
  - [**Aja Frost:** Provide feedback to GrowthX Labs on a potential partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/cU7zx59RqsBoAA53VUsEasrdiL6iUKga?tab=summary&timestamp=2560.0)

action_items.description: Email Aja Lovable guides/templates links + AugmentCode citations; include CheckThat.ai preview
Sync w/ Adam re: next steps; update Marcel
